notes: Wave 1 of the v3 depth pass. Five authored townships (fixed axial hexes, one per quadrant + one near home) and the notice-board bounty deck. Townships override terrain at their hex and open the trading post on arrival. Bounties are the silver income and the destination engine: take a notice, ride to the marked hex, deal with what you find, collect at any board. One active at a time.

townships.name: WET MERCY
townships.arrive: wet mercy. the well is the whole reason for the town, and the town knows it. lamps in four windows.
townships.rumor: word at the rail: '%s' · the barkeep polishes a glass that was already clean.

townships.name: PROVIDENCIA
townships.arrive: providencia. the mission bell is cracked and rings anyway, out of stubbornness or faith · the town has stopped distinguishing.
townships.rumor: an old man at the end of the bar says '%s' and then pretends he didn't.

townships.name: NINEMILE
townships.arrive: ninemile. nine miles from what, nobody says. the boards of the walk are new · the town is younger than its dust.
townships.rumor: the card table goes quiet long enough for somebody to say '%s'.

townships.name: COFFIN FLATS
townships.arrive: coffin flats. the carpenter does the town's only steady trade and keeps the measurements of everyone who's ever bought him a drink.
townships.rumor: the carpenter, without looking up: '%s'. he planes another curl off the board.

townships.name: DOGTOOTH
townships.arrive: dogtooth. close enough to home that the porch dogs know your horse. the store sells everything, mostly rope.
townships.rumor: the storekeep leans on the word like a fencepost: '%s'.

services.hotel_text: the bed is honest and the sheets nearly are. you sleep without dreaming, which out here is a paid service.
services.saloon_text: the whiskey is brown and the water is browner. you feel realigned with the territory's opinion of you.
services.bookstall_text: a dime novel · TRUE TALES OF THE TERRITORY · wrong about the geography, wrong about the sisters, right about one thing in a way the author clearly didn't notice. that is lore, if you can hold it.

bounties.notice: NOTICE · a man out on the bone flats counting ribs that aren't his to count · the town would like him counted · 3 silver
bounties.arrive: he's there, kneeling in the bone, lips moving. he has reached a number no honest count arrives at. he looks up without stopping.

choices.label: bring him in
choices.text: he comes along quietly, still counting. by the second hex he is counting your buttons. the town can have the arithmetic.

choices.label: let him count
choices.text: you leave him to it. some sums want finishing more than towns want justice. he nods you a number as you go · that is lore, if you can hold it.

bounties.notice: NOTICE · a woman selling rain by the jarful out past the salt · the jars are real, the rain is somebody's · 3 silver
bounties.arrive: her cart is ranked with sealed jars, each one raining privately on the inside. 'every jar honest,' she says. 'the question is whose weather it was.'

choices.label: bring her in
choices.text: she comes willingly and sells two jars on the ride. you deliver her, richer in ways the notice didn't cover.

choices.label: buy a jar instead
choices.text: you pay her and she picks the jar for you · 'this one's yours anyway.' it rains a childhood afternoon. you let the bounty spoil.

bounties.notice: NOTICE · a surveyor chaining the scrub with instruments nobody issued · his maps are changing the distances · 4 silver
bounties.arrive: he has a theodolite aimed at nothing and a ledger of bearings that disagree with the ground on purpose. 'the territory is negotiable,' he says. 'i negotiate.'

choices.label: bring him in
choices.text: you fold his tripod for him. the ride back is one hex shorter than the ride out, which you decide not to mention to anybody.

choices.label: read the ledger
choices.text: you make him show you. the bearings spell a sentence about the weave that the manual would rather you hadn't read. you let him wander · that is lore, and it is heavy.

bounties.notice: NOTICE · a man in the gallows wood answering to a dead man's name · the widow is owed either the name back or the man · 3 silver
bounties.arrive: he wears the name like a coat cut for a bigger man. 'it was going spare,' he says. 'names shouldn't sit empty. they let things in.'

choices.label: bring him in
choices.text: he gives the name up a hex outside town · you watch it leave him, and he is smaller and easier to lead after.

choices.label: let him keep it
choices.text: maybe he's right about empty names. you ride off. behind you he says thank you with somebody else's gratitude · grit for that costs, lore for what you saw.

bounties.notice: NOTICE · providencia's second bell went missing before it was ever hung · a rider was seen carrying a shape of quiet · 3 silver
bounties.arrive: the rider has the bell wrapped in wool on a travois · it rings anyway, muffled, on the offbeat of the horse. 'i'm not stealing it,' she says. 'i'm keeping it from saying something.'

choices.label: bring her in
choices.text: bell and thief both. the whole ride back it rings the offbeat, and by town you almost hear the word it was building toward.

choices.label: help her bury it
choices.text: you dig where she says. the quiet afterward has a shape, and the shape is the word unsaid. that is lore · the town is owed an apology you won't be delivering.

bounties.notice: NOTICE · a pistol going door to door on its own recognizance out past ninemile · owner deceased · the gun disagrees · 4 silver
bounties.arrive: it sits on a fence post, hammer politely down, waiting the way dogs wait. whoever it's waiting for is not you, and you both know it.

choices.label: bring it in
choices.text: it rides in your saddlebag without complaint, which is somehow worse. the town locks it in the safe and everyone agrees to say the matter is settled.

choices.label: point it home
choices.text: you turn it on the post to face the widow's house. it considers. by morning it will be on her porch, and that is between them. you keep one of its rounds for your trouble.

bounties.notice: NOTICE · singing heard from the mesa country on nights with no moon · the words arrive before the voices · wet mercy will pay to know · 3 silver
bounties.arrive: you find the choir · five stones in a ring, humming the sisters' motif backward. played backward, it is a lullaby. somebody wove a comfort in under the theology.

choices.label: report it to the board
choices.text: you carry the shape of the song back in your chest. the town writes it down wrong, pays you anyway, and sleeps better for the wrong version.

choices.label: keep it for yourself
choices.text: some songs are owed to the first person who hears them right. you hum it on the ride and your grit settles like dust after wind.

bounties.notice: NOTICE · a funeral procession crossing the dust for nine days · no mourners, no deceased, one empty box · somebody should ask it to stop · 4 silver
bounties.arrive: the procession walks itself · six pallbearers of heat-shimmer under an empty box, patient as noon. it is rehearsing, you understand suddenly, and it has not yet been told for whom.

choices.label: turn it back
choices.text: you ride ahead of it and stop your horse crosswise, the way you'd turn cattle. it considers you a long moment · measuring · then folds up like heat does at dusk. you don't tell the town what it was measuring.

choices.label: walk with it a while
choices.text: you fall in behind the box, hat off. after a mile it stops on its own, satisfied · all it wanted was one mourner willing. what you learned about the empty box is lore, and you'd give it back if you could.
